name: jtbd-odi
description: An end-to-end Outcome-Driven Innovation (Tony Ulwick / Strategyn) toolkit. Runs every phase of an ODI engagement — job definition, job map, outcome extraction & netting, outcome validation, screener, survey design, opportunity scoring (with WTP analysis), segmentation, competitive analysis, Growth Strategy Matrix posture, value prop, roadmap, engineering specs, ODI Canvas, and a packaged deliverable bundle containing exactly the six Table 30.1 artifacts. Strictly faithful to the v2.4.2 practitioner handbook. Includes an autonomous synthetic-respondent layer (Reddit/X/forum/review mining + virtual personas + simulated survey responses) for hypothesis generation only, with explicit guardrails against replacing real human surveys.

method.school: Ulwick (quantitative ODI). Explicitly NOT Christensen Jobs-as-Progress.
method.opportunity_algorithm: Opportunity = Importance + max(Importance - Satisfaction, 0), top-2-box on a 1-5 scale, normalized 0-10
method.twenty_percent_rule: To win share on an underserved outcome, the new product must satisfy it ~20% better than the best competitor (Ch 22, Ch 28).

orchestrators.runfullodi: Master orchestrator. real or rehearsal mode. 4-8 weeks (real). Decision-grade.
orchestrators.researchpath: Online research replaces interviews; REAL survey provides decision-grade scoring. 3-5 weeks.
orchestrators.runliteodi: Qualitative-only (no survey). Directional, not decision-grade. 2-3 weeks.
orchestrators.exportdeliverables: Final bundler. Refuses to ship without all six Table 30.1 artifacts.
orchestrators.whatdoido: Reverse lookup — 'I have X file, what command?'.
orchestrators.preflight: Should you even do ODI? Ch 4 checklist.
orchestrators.demo: See realistic outputs from a worked example (Bosch CS20). No commitment.
orchestrators.brainstormjob: Discover the underlying job for an idea / market / product you don't understand.

newcomer_docs.QUICKSTART.md: 2-minute intro. The 4 entry-point commands side by side. Read this first.
newcomer_docs.GUIDE.md: 10-minute newcomer journey. The 3 paths, every command, every dependency.
newcomer_docs.GLOSSARY.md: Every ODI term translated into plain English.

newcomer_entry_points.odihelp: I don't know what to do
newcomer_entry_points.demo: Show me what this produces — no commitment
newcomer_entry_points.whatdoido: I have a file / data — what command should I run?
newcomer_entry_points.preflight: Is ODI even the right method for me?

guardrails.synthetic_data_warning: All outputs from /mineoutcomes, /hypothesizecomplexity, /sentimentlandscape, and /run-synthetic-survey are HYPOTHESIS-GENERATION accelerators only. They MUST NOT replace the real human survey (n=300-600) required by the guide (Part IV, Chapter 16, Appendix A). Every synthetic deliverable is stamped with a SYNTHETIC banner and a 'next: validate with n>=300 real humans' instruction. /generatevalueprop, /buildroadmap, and /exportdeliverables refuse to be chained off synthetic results without that validation.
guardrails.no_christensen_drift: Outputs must remain Ulwick-faithful: strict outcome syntax, top-2-box, opportunity formula, outcome-based segmentation, job-map anchoring. The plugin refuses to produce 'milkshake-style' narrative jobs or persona-as-segment outputs.
guardrails.no_discrete_celebration: If /choosestrategy places the project in the Discrete cell, output a 'this is a trap — exit' warning rather than a roadmap (Ch 23).
guardrails.validation_gate: /generatesurvey is hard-gated on /validateoutcomes verdict != 'fail'. /exportdeliverables refuses to ship if any of the six Table 30.1 artifacts is missing.
